titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/255

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=240 status=live modified=2017-04-03 15:24:18.030767+00 | translation moa=241 status=live modified=2017-04-03 15:24:22.190999+00

div.p:
Willelmus dei gratia abbas de sancta cruce ⁊ conuentus eiusdem domus ominbus fidelibus salutem. Sciant tam posteri quam presentes . nos concessisse ⁊ hac
carta nostra confirmasse . Domine Aufrice filie eadgari totam terram nostram quam habemus ex donatione patris sui in territorio de dalgarnoc tenendam de no
bis in tota uita sua . reddendo inde nobis primis quatuor annis annuatim unam marcam argenti . medietatem ad festum sancti martini . ⁊
medietatem ad pentecosten . post quatuor uero annos ad eosdem terminos in tota uita sua uiginti solidos. Quod si forte predicta terra
inculta fuerit ⁊ uasta nobis unam marcam argenti ad prefatos terminos quamdiu illa terra uasta fuerit ⁊ inculta . predicta . A . annuatim persoluet .
[damaged parchment] hec conuentio facta est Anno ab incarnatione domini millesimo ducentesimo uicesimo primo . ⁊ in testimonium huius conuentionis sigillum nostrum
[damaged parchment] et sigillo predicte aufrice presenti scripto apposuimus . Teste conuentu .

William, by the grace of God abbot of Holyrood, and the convent of the same house, to all the faithful, Greeting: Let both future and present know that we have granted, and by this our charter made firm, to the lady Affrica, daughter of Edgar, our whole land that we have from the donation of her father in the territory of Dalgarnock, to be held of us for the whole of her life, by returning thereupon to us annually for the first four years one merk of silver, half at the feast of St Martin, and half at Pentecost, but after four years, at the same terms for the whole of her life, twenty shillings. If, however, the said land should be uncultivated or waste, the foresaid Affrica should pay us one silver merk at the forementioned terms for as long as that land should be waste or uncultivated. This agreement was made in the year of the Incarnation of the Lord 1221, and in testimony of this agreement we affixed our seal and the seal of the foresaid Affrica to the present document.
